title: From Training to Simulation to Real-Time Feedback: A GPU Tour of Infleqtion’s Q4Bio Project Toward Quantum-Enabled Biomarker Discovery | Infleqtion
why:
- Infleqtion is delivering a hybrid quantum-classical biomarker discovery platform that identifies compact, high-impact feature sets from complex multimodal cancer data, capturing higher-order correlations that are difficult for conventional methods.
- Their Q4Bio project utilizes hybrid quantum computing that leverages GPU acceleration alongside their Sqale quantum processing unit (QPU), enabling faster model discovery and training.
- The approach includes GPU-accelerated Instantaneous Quantum Polynomial (IQP) circuit optimization, allowing for rapid training of quantum neural networks relevant to biomarker discovery, scalable to thousands of qubits.
- Infleqtion has used GPU resources, including NVIDIA GH200 Grace Hopper superchips and 24,000 NVIDIA A100 GPU node hours on the Perlmutter supercomputer, integrating CUDA-Q for simulation and validation.
- Their workflow combines GPU-accelerated simulation via CUDA-Q to estimate both ideal and hardware-noisy outcomes, supporting an end-to-end pipeline from model training to real QPU execution with 12 logical qubits demonstrated on their neutral atom quantum computer.
